_description: Drop ONE entry into your MCP client to auto-wire all 13 arifOS constitutional tools. Pick the transport that matches your setup.

context7._comment: Context7 — Up-to-date library docs for LLMs. Remote HTTP, no API key needed.
context7._use_when: Agent needs current docs for any library (fastmcp, pydantic, playwright, etc.). Call resolve-library-id first, then get-library-docs.

codegraphcontext._comment: CodeGraphContext — Codebase graph analysis. Local stdio via pip cgc.
codegraphcontext._use_when: Agent needs to understand call chains, class hierarchies, cross-module relationships in arifOS code.

arifos-remote._comment: RECOMMENDED — Zero install. Connects to live arifOS MCP over HTTPS. 13 tools, always latest.
arifos-remote._use_when: You want to use arifOS without installing anything. Just add this entry.

arifos-local._comment: Local stdio via pip. Install: pip install arifos (v2026.3.7 on PyPI)
arifos-local._use_when: Privacy-first or offline. Runs on your own machine, no external network.

arifos-npm._comment: Node/npm — TypeScript client SDK + MCP transport. Run: npm install @arifos/mcp
arifos-npm._use_when: You're in a JS/TS project and want typed arifOS client methods (anchorSession, reasonMind, etc.).
arifos-npm._note: @arifos/mcp is a CABLE (L2 adapter), not the kernel. All governance runs server-side in Python.
arifos-npm._sdk_usage: import { createClient, ENDPOINTS } from '@arifos/mcp'; const client = await createClient({ transport: 'http', endpoint: ENDPOINTS.VPS });

arifos-docker._comment: Official Docker image from Docker Hub — ariffazil/arifos:latest
arifos-docker._use_when: Isolated execution without polluting your Python environment.

arifos-dev._comment: Developer mode — from cloned repo with venv. Run: git clone https://github.com/ariffazil/arifOS && pip install -e '.[dev]'
arifos-dev._use_when: You cloned the repo and want to hack on arifOS itself.
